evidence.description: CRIT-001..CRIT-008 each cite a concrete .dom.vitest.ts file path, named data-testid attributes, and explicit spy-count assertions tied to REQ-EDIT-XXX and PROP-EDIT-XXX (lines 8-47). PROP-EDIT traceability table in Â§4 (lines 244-262) maps every integration-tier obligation to a CRIT entry. Sprint 1 pure-core scope is explicitly out-of-scope (Â§3, lines 222-228; Â§6, line 310-311).

CRIT-002 enumerates at-threshold/one-ms-before/rapid-burst (line 16-17); CRIT-003 enumerates blur-while-dirty/saving/clean/no-duplicate (line 20-22); CRIT-004 covers all four FsError kinds and banner presence/absence across all 5 statuses (line 25-27); CRIT-005 enumerates non-empty/whitespace-only/disabled-states branches (line 30-32); CRIT-006 enumerates explicit-button/ctrlKey/metaKey/out-of-pane (line 35-37); CRIT-007 enumerates all 5 status DOM attribute matrices (line 40-42).

CRIT-009..CRIT-012 (lines 49-67) test each adapter's exact invoke command name + payload shape, EditorStateChannel listen/unlisten lifecycle, debounceTimer setTimeout/clearTimeout via injected clock, keyboardListener panelRoot scoping (NOT document) with preventDefault, and clipboardAdapter.write(body) routing â€” each binary-evaluable with named spy and grep assertions.

CRIT-013 (lines 69-72) names the 5-layer Deep Shadow string, #dd5b00 accent, 8px radius, 15px/600 button typography as exact grep targets in EditorPane.svelte source â€” concrete, falsifiable design audit.

Iter-1 FIND-002 fully addressed: Â§1 Sprint Goal (line 88) explicitly names two separate commands ('bun test src/lib/editor' and 'bun run test:dom -- src/lib/editor/__tests__/dom'); Â§4 Run commands (lines 278-281) explicitly states 'There is no single command that runs both tiers; both commands must be run and pass independently.' CRIT-008 pass threshold (line 47) and DoD #1 (line 323) both require both commands. No 'bun run test' (non-existent script) reference remains in convergence-relevant sections.

Weight sum verified: 0.07+0.10+0.10+0.11+0.08+0.09+0.08+0.06+0.06+0.05+0.05+0.05+0.05+0.05 = 1.00 (cited at line 304 and confirmed independently). 14 CRITs are non-overlapping â€” CRIT-001..CRIT-008 cover EditorPane DOM behavior, CRIT-009..CRIT-012 cover adapter unit-level wiring, CRIT-013..CRIT-014 cover structural/purity audits.

Iter-1 FIND-001 fully addressed: all 5 adapter unit test files now live under '__tests__/dom/' with the '.dom.vitest.ts' suffix (lines 266-276). vitest.config.ts include pattern 'src/lib/**/__tests__/dom/**/*.vitest.ts' (verified at promptnotes/vitest.config.ts:22) matches this naming. CRIT-009..CRIT-012 pass thresholds (lines 52, 57, 62, 67) reference the exact '__tests__/dom/<name>.dom.vitest.ts' paths. The vi.mock/vi.mocked API surface used by these tests is now consistent with the runner that will discover them (vitest).

package.json (line 12) defines 'test:dom': 'vitest run', so 'bun run test:dom -- src/lib/editor/__tests__/dom' is a valid invocation. 'bun test src/lib/editor' uses bun's built-in runner which auto-discovers '*.test.ts' but not '*.vitest.ts' (per vitest.config.ts comment at lines 19-21), so the two commands cleanly partition the test universe with no double-counting and no orphan files.

CRIT-014 (lines 74-77) preserves the Phase 5 canonical purity-audit grep on the three Sprint 1 pure-core files plus the svelte/store import ban (PROP-EDIT-036) â€” verification-tier obligations carried into the contract.
